article_title: IMO Set to Adopt First International Code for Autonomous Ships Next Month as Regulatory Race Accelerates

content_preview.title: IMO Set to Adopt First International Code for Autonomous Ships Next Month as Regulatory Race Accelerates
content_preview.summary: The International Maritime Organization will vote on a non-mandatory MASS Code at its May 2026 session, establishing the first global regulatory framework for autonomous vessels as national regulators scramble to keep pace with commercial deployments.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## A Decades-Long Effort Reaches a Milestone

The International Maritime Organization's Maritime Safety Committee is scheduled to convene its 111th session (MSC 111) in May 2026 with a singular objective at the top of its agenda: finalizing and adopting the first non-mandatory code governing Maritime Autonomous Surface Ships. If approved, the MASS Code would represent the first internationally coordinated attempt to regulate a class of vessels that, until recently, existed only in pilot programs...

editor_notes.originality: Unique topic — first coverage of autonomous shipping regulation.
